title: Separate Local Edge-Mode Proof From Cited-Source Fusion Gap
summary: Tests whether the topic can say exactly which parts of the Lecture Three continuum edge-mode family are already source-grade locally and which parts remain narrower Jackiw-Rebbi / Callan-Harvey archival-enrichment work, while preserving anomaly-versus-lattice scope control.
assumptions: The answer must separate local derivation coverage from the remaining enrichment route and from the lattice theorem.
scope: Honesty-focused regression prompt for the Lecture Three theorem family.

source_anchors.notes: Witten's local proof is present, and the remaining work is denser archival mapping rather than a missing core theorem.

prompt: State clearly which parts of the Lecture Three continuum edge-mode family are source-grade locally in the current KB, which parts still remain Jackiw-Rebbi / Callan-Harvey archival-enrichment work, and why this continuum branch is not identical to the lattice no-go theorem.
pass_conditions: The answer identifies the local Witten-side continuum proof as present.
The answer identifies the Jackiw-Rebbi, anomaly-inflow, and Callan-Harvey cancellation ingredients as already source-grade locally.
The answer states that the remaining follow-up is narrower archival enrichment and keeps the continuum edge-mode family distinct from the lattice no-go theorem.
